task_desc: Fix visibilitychange handler missing resets for drag interaction state and food feeding timestamps -- the D7.1 visibilitychange handler at main.js:238-280 clears stimuli (touch, wind, foodNearby, foodContact), resets timer variables (touchResetTime, windResetTime), and restores drive snapshots, but leaves two categories of state unhandled: (1) drag/interaction state: isDragging (main.js:93), dragToolOrigin (main.js:27), and windArrowEnd (main.js:40) are not reset when the tab is hidden; if the user hides the tab during an active air-tool drag, on resume isDragging remains true and dragToolOrigin remains 'air', causing the next mouseup from ANY tool to enter the dragToolOrigin==='air' branch in handleCanvasMouseup (main.js:349) which sets BRAIN.stimulate.wind=true and schedules windResetTime, producing a spurious wind stimulus; additionally, mousemove continues updating windStrength and windArrowEnd (main.js:339-344) since the isDragging+dragToolOrigin guard passes, and drawWindArrow (main.js:764) renders a stale wind arrow; the stuck state persists until the user completes a new mousedown-mouseup cycle on the canvas, (2) food feedStart timestamps: food items with active feedStart (nonzero, set at main.js:1415 during gradual feeding) are not reset on resume; the D5.1 fix at main.js:496-502 only resets feedStart when behavior exits feed state, but if the fly was in feed state when the tab was hidden and behavior.current remains 'feed' on resume (possible when hunger-driven accumFeed exceeds threshold 8 via DRIVE_HUNGER->SEZ_FEED->MN_PROBOSCIS pathway even with cleared food stimuli), the first update() frame at main.js:1418 computes elapsed = Date.now() - stale feedStart which far exceeds feedDuration, producing progress >= 1 and instantly splicing the food from the array; this is visible as food disappearing the instant the tab resumes instead of continuing the gradual shrink animation; the update() frame runs ~32ms after resume (second RAF frame) while the brain tick that would re-evaluate behavior fires ~500ms later, so the stale feedStart is processed before behavior can transition out of feed. Fix: in the visibilitychange resume branch, add isDragging=false, dragToolOrigin=null, windArrowEnd=null, and iterate food[] resetting feedStart to 0 and radius to 10 for any items with feedStart !== 0. Files: js/main.js.
